title: Physics-Informed Deep Learning-Based Proof-of-Concept Study of a Novel Elastohydrodynamic Seal for Supercritical CO2 Turbomachinery
authors_or_org: Lyathakula, K.R. (North Carolina State University); Cesmeci, S.; DeMond, M.; Hassan, M.F. (Georgia Southern University); Xu, H.; Tang, J. (Ultool, LLC)
claim_supported: Proposes and evaluates (via a physics-informed deep-learning surrogate) a novel elastohydrodynamic shaft-end seal design for sCO2 turbomachinery, targeting the leakage/seal-life bottleneck identified across other sCO2 turbine records in this lane. Journal of Energy Resources Technology, Vol 145 Iss 12, art 121705, published 2023-12-01 (online 2023-09-29).
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1115/1.4063326) confirmed journal-article, container-title Journal of Energy Resources Technology, matching author list and volume/issue/page.
notes: New sub-niche vs. existing ledger: seal/leakage design (existing L04 records cover recuperators, reactors, and general reviews but no seal-specific primary paper). Full text is ASME-paywalled; identity confirmed via direct Crossref API fetch this session, not via full-text read.

title: Performance analysis of a 100 kW S-CO2 centrifugal compressor: Design and experimental study
authors_or_org: Yang, K.; Zhang, N.; Chen, L.; Qin, Z.; Wang, L.; Dong, K.; Li, H.; Li, X.; Liu, H.; Song, Z.; Fang, Z.; Wang, X.; Qiao, X. (Shanghai Marine Diesel Engine Institute, China)
claim_supported: Design and experimental study of a 100 kW-class S-CO2 centrifugal compressor built at the Shanghai Marine Diesel Engine Institute test loop; per independent trade-summary corroboration the unit reached stable operation at 40,000 rpm with isentropic efficiency of 62.71% and pressure ratio 1.634. Energy, Vol 322, art 135696, May 2025.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1016/j.energy.2025.135696) confirmed journal-article, container-title Energy, matching author list; full text paywalled, not independently read this session.
notes: New China-geography compressor-hardware primary paper (existing ledger's Chinese sCO2 primary sources are mostly national-lab press releases, e.g. L04-029, not compressor component papers). Numeric performance figures sourced from independent WebSearch trade-summary cross-corroboration, not directly read from the paywalled article; verifier should confirm before treating as load-bearing.

title: Experimental investigation on performance test of 150-kW-class supercritical CO2 centrifugal compressor
authors_or_org: Park, J.H.; Cha, J.E.; Lee, S.W. (Korea Institute of Energy Research, KIER)
claim_supported: KIER-designed 150-kW-class S-CO2 centrifugal compressor for a 500 kW waste-heat-recovery power generation system; design-point pressure ratio ~1.75-1.76 and isentropic efficiency of 80% at 36,000 rpm, confirmed across repeated tests. Applied Thermal Engineering, Vol 210, art 118310, June 2022.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1016/j.applthermaleng.2022.118310) confirmed journal-article, container-title Applied Thermal Engineering; authors and KIER affiliation cross-corroborated via independent WebSearch (ResearchGate/CoLab listings).
notes: Complements existing KAERI/KAIST/POSTECH sCO2 break-even record (L04-028) with a distinct South Korean national-lab (KIER) component-level compressor primary paper -- a different institution within Korea's sCO2 ecosystem. Full text paywalled; not independently read this session.

title: Experimental study on NOx emission characteristics under oxy-fuel combustion
authors_or_org: Wu, H.; An, Z.; Zhang, K.; Mao, Y.; Zheng, Z.; Liu, Z. (State Key Laboratory of Coal Combustion, Huazhong University of Science and Technology, Wuhan, China)
claim_supported: Experimental measurement of NOx emission characteristics under oxy-fuel combustion on a 3 MWth test platform plus a laboratory-scale platform, compared against air combustion -- directly relevant to Allam-cycle/direct-fired sCO2 combustor NOx behavior (oxy-fuel, not air-fuel, combustion). Clean Energy, Vol 7 Iss 3, pp 595-601, 2023.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1093/ce/zkad007) confirmed journal-article, container-title Clean Energy (Oxford University Press); HUST State Key Laboratory of Coal Combustion affiliation confirmed via independent WebSearch of co-author Zhaohui Liu's research profile.
notes: Fills the Allam-cycle-combustor/oxy-fuel-NOx sub-niche flagged as a research gap in the existing ledger's L04-013 abstract ("scientific literature currently lacks extensive research on the oxy-fuel combustion process... with the Allam cycle"). This is a coal-combustion-context oxy-fuel NOx study, not gas-turbine-specific -- relevance to Allam-cycle combustors is adjacent/analogous, not identical; verifier should weigh this distinction.

title: Thermostructural analysis on airfoil fin printed circuit heat exchanger using supercritical CO2
authors_or_org: Raji, A.P.; Ranganathan, S.; Arputharaj, B.S.; Raja, V. (Kumaraguru College of Technology, Coimbatore, Tamil Nadu, India)
claim_supported: Thermal-structural (stress/thermal-cycling) analysis of an airfoil-fin printed-circuit-heat-exchanger (PCHE) design in sCO2 service, addressing PCHE durability under high-pressure/high-temperature operation -- directly relevant to the PCHE thermal-cycling bottleneck already flagged in this lane (L04-004/005/006/029). Journal of Thermal Analysis and Calorimetry, Vol 149 Iss 9, pp 4153-4177, published online 2024-03-15.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1007/s10973-024-12925-y) confirmed journal-article, container-title Journal of Thermal Analysis and Calorimetry; Kumaraguru College of Technology (Dept. of Aeronautical Engineering) affiliation confirmed via independent WebSearch of co-author Arul Prakash Raji's ResearchGate profile.
notes: First India-geography sCO2-hardware primary technical paper in this lane's academic set (existing India records, L04-024/037, are conference-proceedings/government pages, not a structural-analysis primary paper). Full text paywalled; not independently read this session.

title: Experimental Study on a Supercritical CO2 Centrifugal Compressor Used in a MWe Scale Power Cycle
authors_or_org: Zhu, Y.; Liang, S.; Guo, C.; Guo, Y.; Li, Z.; Gong, X.; Jiang, Y. (Institute of Engineering Thermophysics, Chinese Academy of Sciences)
claim_supported: Experimental performance testing of a supercritical CO2 centrifugal compressor scaled for a MWe-class sCO2 power cycle, conducted by the CAS Institute of Engineering Thermophysics (IET-CAS) -- the same institute behind the recuperator-thermal-inertia record already in this lane (L04-029), here extended to compressor hardware. Applied Sciences, Vol 13 Iss 1, art 385, published 2022-12-28.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.3390/app13010385) confirmed journal-article, container-title Applied Sciences (MDPI); IET-CAS affiliation confirmed via independent WebSearch of co-author Yuming Zhu's institutional profile (sciprofiles.com).
notes: Adds MWe-scale (as opposed to 100-150 kW-class) Chinese compressor test data, broadening the compressor-hardware sub-niche coverage started by L04-102/103 in this top-up batch.

title: Radioisotope Thermophotovoltaic Generator Design Methods and Performance Estimates for Space Missions
authors_or_org: Wang, X.; Liang, R.; Fisher, P.; Chan, W.; Xu, J. (Massachusetts Institute of Technology)
claim_supported: Presents design methodology for a radioisotope thermophotovoltaic (RTPV) generator using two-dimensional micropatterned photonic-crystal emitters and spectral control, positioned as a higher-conversion-efficiency, lower-mass alternative to conventional radioisotope thermoelectric generators (RTGs) for space missions. Journal of Propulsion and Power, Vol 36 Iss 4, pp 593-603, July 2020.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.2514/1.B37623) confirmed journal-article, container-title Journal of Propulsion and Power (AIAA); MIT affiliation corroborated via the MIT DSpace open-access mirror of this article found in WebSearch.
notes: New sub-niche: space-power / radioisotope TPV, distinct from the grid-scale thermal-battery TPV literature already in this lane (L04-001/002/008/009/010). Relevant to founder-adjacent aerospace power applications of the same core TPV technology; full text paywalled behind AIAA ARC, not independently read this session.

title: High-temperature Pumping of Silicon for Thermal Energy Grid Storage
authors_or_org: Amy, C.; Pishahang, M.; Kelsall, C.C.; LaPotin, A.; Henry, A. (Massachusetts Institute of Technology)
claim_supported: Demonstrates a mechanical pump design capable of moving molten silicon at very high temperature (silicon melts at ~1414C) for grid-scale thermal-energy storage, a key enabling sub-component for silicon-based, TPV-discharged thermal-battery concepts -- authored by the same MIT (Asegun Henry) group behind the foundational 40%-efficiency TPV cell record already in this lane (L04-001). Energy, Vol 233, art 121105, 2021.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1016/j.energy.2021.121105) confirmed journal-article, container-title Energy, author list (Amy, Pishahang, Kelsall, LaPotin, Henry) matching.
notes: Fills a balance-of-plant sub-niche not yet covered in this lane: the molten-heat-transfer-fluid pumping hardware underlying silicon-based thermal-battery/TPV systems (distinct from the liquid-tin pump referenced only via vendor claim in L04-052 for Fourth Power). Full text paywalled; not independently read this session.

title: Numerical Study on Rotor Cooling of Turbine in Supercritical Carbon Dioxide Cycle
authors_or_org: Jiang, P.; Tian, Y.; Wang, B.; Guo, C. (Institute of Engineering Thermophysics, Chinese Academy of Sciences, Beijing)
claim_supported: Numerical (CFD) study of rotor cooling schemes for sCO2 cycle turbines operating at high turbine-inlet temperature, addressing blade/rotor thermal-management -- a materials/thermal-management bottleneck adjacent to the 500-715C turbine-inlet-temperature gap already flagged in this lane (L04-026, L04-039). ACS Omega, Vol 7 Iss 43, pp 39325-39334, published 2022-10-20.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1021/acsomega.2c05531) confirmed journal-article, container-title ACS Omega; IET-CAS Beijing affiliation confirmed via independent WebSearch of the article's institutional listing.
notes: Third IET-CAS primary paper in this top-up batch (alongside L04-106), reinforcing that this specific CAS institute is a recurring, identifiable Chinese sCO2-turbomachinery research center across compressor, turbine, and recuperator sub-topics.

title: Large Area Near-Field Thermophotovoltaics for Low Temperature Applications
authors_or_org: Selvidge, J.; France, R.M.; Goldsmith, J.; Solanki, P.; Steiner, M.A.; Tervo, E.J. (National Renewable Energy Laboratory, NREL)
claim_supported: NREL-led large-area near-field TPV device using an epitaxial co-fabrication approach (0.28 cm^2 self-supported emitter-cell pair, 150 nm gap) generates 1.22 mW at only 460C -- a 25-fold power-density increase over the same cell measured in a far-field configuration, opening a lower-temperature (waste-heat-relevant, not 1,500-2,400C) TPV pathway distinct from the LaPotin/Roy-Layinde high-temperature tandem/air-bridge cells already in this lane (L04-001, L04-002). Advanced Materials, Vol 37 Iss 5, art 2411524, online 2024-12-15 / print Feb 2025.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1002/adma.202411524) confirmed journal-article, container-title Advanced Materials; NREL authorship and the 460C/1.22 mW/25-fold figures corroborated via independent WebSearch synthesis of the NREL/OSTI-hosted preprint (docs.nrel.gov/docs/fy25osti/90658.pdf) describing the same work.
notes: Directly addresses this lane's TPV-emitter-durability bottleneck from the opposite direction (near-field, low-temperature, vs. far-field/very-high-temperature) -- a materially different technical approach worth flagging alongside L04-009's materials-screening angle.

title: A comprehensive evaluation of the effect of different control valves on the dynamic performance of a recompression supercritical CO2 Brayton cycle
authors_or_org: Bian, X.; Wang, X.; Wang, R.; Cai, J.; Tian, H.; Shu, G.; Lin, Z.; Yu, X.; Shi, L. (Tianjin University, State Key Laboratory of Engines)
claim_supported: Tianjin University dynamic (MATLAB/Simulink, 'Jing Wei' component-library) model study evaluating how different individual control valves (turbine bypass valve, high-temperature-recuperator bypass valve, inventory-control valves) affect the transient/load-following performance of a recompression sCO2 Brayton cycle; identifies the turbine bypass and HTR bypass valves as the best-performing control elements. Energy, Vol 248, art 123630, 2022.
locator: Crossref metadata record (api.crossref.org/works/10.1016/j.energy.2022.123630) confirmed journal-article, container-title Energy, matching author list including Gequn Shu (Tianjin University); valve-performance findings cross-corroborated via independent WebSearch summary of the same paper.
notes: A component-level (individual-valve) primary paper on turbomachinery/BOP controls, distinct from the existing Chinese control-strategy review already in the ledger (L04-022) and the dynamic-characterization primary paper L04-007 -- both of those address system-level strategy/dynamics rather than valve-by-valve component comparison.

title: E139-24 Standard Test Methods for Conducting Creep, Creep-Rupture, and Stress-Rupture Tests of Metallic Materials
claim_supported: Current active edition (E0139-24, active as of 2025-03-18, superseding E0139-11R18/E0139-11/E0139-06/E0139-00) of the standard test method for creep, creep-rupture, and stress-rupture testing of metallic materials under constant tensile load at constant elevated temperature -- the qualification-testing standard applicable to nickel/titanium alloys used in sCO2, TPV-adjacent, and advanced-reactor turbomachinery components operating above 650C.
locator: ASTM store product page (store.astm.org/standards/e139) fetched directly this session, listing designation E0139-24, DOI 10.1520/E0139-24, developing subcommittee E28.04, 14 pages, Book of Standards Vol 03.01.
notes: Fills the standards-gap sub-niche flagged in the existing ledger (L04-030 found no sCO2-specific ASME Performance Test Code): this is a general (not sCO2-specific) but directly applicable alloy-qualification test standard, relevant context for the same certification-gap bottleneck. Standard text itself is paywalled ($80); only the product/metadata page was fetched, not the full standard body.

title: Progress in Overcoming Materials Challenges with Supercritical CO2 Recompression Closed Brayton Cycles
authors_or_org: Walker, M.S.; Kruizenga, A.M.; Weck, P.F.; Withey, E.A.; Fleming, D.D.; Rochau, G.E. (Sandia National Laboratories)
claim_supported: Sandia National Laboratories technical report (SAND-2016-8126, DOE Office of Nuclear Energy-funded) documenting progress on five named sCO2 Brayton-cycle materials challenges: low-cost alloys for cooler sections, materials qualification for 10 MW-class systems, turbine (blade/disk) degradation, gas-foil-bearing performance in a CO2 environment, and gas-chemistry effects on alloy corrosion resistance.
locator: OSTI.GOV bibliographic record (osti.gov/biblio/1561836) fetched directly this session; confirmed title, six named authors, report number SAND-2016-8126/646784, publication date 2016-06-01, and abstract text.
notes: This 2016 report's named bearings challenge foreshadows a still-open, more recent Sandia industry solicitation found this session -- 'Collaborative Opportunities with Sandia National Laboratories for Supercritical CO2 Power Cycle Bearings' (sam.gov/workspace/contract/opp/bf1c094c70638183d5f208d29b6e9149/view), seeking vendor bearing-design/manufacturing capability -- but that SAM.gov listing is a JavaScript-rendered page that returned no extractable text via direct fetch this session (only WebSearch-synthesized corroboration, including a named Sandia point of contact and phone number, was obtainable), so it is NOT included as a separate record to avoid an unfetched/rejectable entry; noted here only as corroborating context for a future scout with browser-level SAM.gov access.

title: LPO Announces Conditional Commitment for Long Duration Compressed Air Energy Storage to Enable a Diverse and Reliable Generation Mix
authors_or_org: US Department of Energy, Office of Energy Dominance Financing (EDF, formerly Loan Programs Office)
claim_supported: DOE Loan Programs Office (announced 2025-01-08) issued a conditional commitment for a loan guarantee of up to $1.76 billion (including up to $279 million capitalized interest) to GEM A-CAES, LLC for the Willow Rock Energy Storage Center in Kern County, California -- an advanced compressed-air energy storage (A-CAES) system providing 4,000 MWh / 500 MW of long-duration storage at an estimated 80% round-trip efficiency, which captures and reuses compression heat and passes air through a turbine to generate power on discharge.
locator: Article body, energy.gov/edf/articles/lpo-announces-conditional-commitment-long-duration-compressed-air-energy-storage, fetched directly this session.
notes: New sub-niche: a heat-capture/turbine-expansion balance-of-plant technology (A-CAES) adjacent to but distinct from sCO2/TPV/ORC already covered in this lane, financed via a large federal loan-guarantee instrument -- a concrete government financing trigger for BOP turbomachinery at storage scale.

title: Fervo Energy Secures $421 Million in Non-Recourse Project Financing for Cape Station
claim_supported: Fervo Energy announced (2026-03-19) $421 million in non-recourse project debt for its Cape Station enhanced-geothermal project (Beaver County, Utah) -- $309M construction-to-term loan, $61M tax-credit bridge loan, $51M letter-of-credit facility -- led by Barclays, BBVA, HSBC, MUFG, RBC, and Societe Generale, targeting ~100 MW operating capacity by early 2027 and scaling to 500 MW, fully contracted via PPAs with Southern California Edison, Shell Energy, and community-choice aggregators.
locator: Press release body, fervoenergy.com/fervo-energy-secures-421-million-in-non-recourse-project-financing-for-cape-station, fetched directly this session.
notes: Primary company press release confirming a specific, named, dollar-denominated project-financing commitment for the same Cape Station project already tied to a 1.75 GW Turboden ORC turbine order in this lane (L04-044) -- direct evidence that the turbine-procurement commitment is now matched by committed non-recourse project debt, strengthening the bankability signal for ORC/EGS power-block hardware. Tiered T1 by analogy to existing accepted company-press-release records in this lane (L04-045, L04-049, L04-050), consistent with source-standards treatment of a primary company announcement of a concrete, named financial/procurement commitment.
